message:
feat(backup): implement zstd compression for scheduled backups (ft-akx00.3.3)

Scheduled backups previously warned "compression is not implemented" when
compress=true. Now they actually compress database.db → database.db.zst
using zstd level 3 after export, with graceful fallback to uncompressed
backup on failure.

Changes:
- backup.rs: add `compressed: bool` field to BackupManifest (#[serde(default)])
- backup.rs: add compress_backup_dir() — zstd compress, remove original,
  update checksums and manifest
- main.rs: call compress_backup_dir() after export when compress=true,
  replace warning with info log
- proptest_backup.rs: update arb_backup_manifest() strategy for new field
- 3 new tests: roundtrip compression+decompression, missing-db error,
  backward-compat manifest deserialization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-akx00.3","title":"[EPIC] Complete operator-support and persistence utility placeholders","description":"Own audited placeholders in operator-support and persistence-adjacent utility surfaces: clock/timestamp generation, bootstrap logging, and scheduled backup compression.

These are not glamorous gaps, but they directly affect auditability, diagnostics, and the operator experience during restore and backup workflows.","design":"Prefer consolidation around shared runtime utilities:
- one canonical timestamp formatter instead of ad hoc stubs,
- one real bootstrap/logging path instead of a nominal crate with no behavior,
- one measurable backup compression pipeline with explicit failure handling.","acceptance_criteria":"- No audited operator-support stub remains.
- Logging/bootstrap behavior is real and documented.
- Backup compression is either implemented end-to-end or replaced by an explicitly narrower supported policy with code and tests.
- Every concrete child bead under this epic closes with comprehensive unit tests plus a dedicated or explicitly assigned E2E/integration scenario, all executed under the shared verification contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"Primary files: `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`, `frankenterm/env-bootstrap/src/lib.rs`, `frankenterm/env-bootstrap/src/ringlog.rs`.

## Track Verification Emphasis
- startup/bootstrap/backup scripts with artifact manifests, timestamp snapshots, and restore or restart proof.
- Heavy validation runs in this track must capture structured logs, exact command transcripts, and artifact paths.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:56.176935Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:29.448991Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backup","e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","startup","stub-audit","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.3","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:56.176935Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2743,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.3","author":"jemanuel","text":"These gaps are easy to deprioritize because they are peripheral. That is exactly why they linger. This track exists to turn them into explicit product behavior instead of quietly approximate infrastructure.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.3.1","title":"Replace chrono_stub_now with a canonical timestamp service","description":"Replace the audited `chrono_stub_now()` helper in `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs`, which currently hand-rolls an approximate timestamp string and labels itself as a stub.

The goal is one canonical time-formatting path for operator-visible timestamps, backup metadata, and any machine-readable surfaces that currently rely on the stub.","design":"1. Trace all callers of `chrono_stub_now()` and related ad hoc timestamp formatting.
2. Choose the canonical timestamp representation(s) needed by the CLI and persistence layer.
3. Replace the stub with a real implementation using the project's preferred time facilities.
4. Add tests for format stability and UTC/local expectations where relevant.","acceptance_criteria":"- `chrono_stub_now()` is removed or renamed into a real non-stub utility with accurate behavior.
- Timestamp output is deterministic and documented for the relevant command surfaces.
- Callers no longer depend on approximate hand-built date/time strings.
- Tests protect the chosen formatting contract.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover the happy path, edge/boundary cases, explicit error/failure/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability relevant to this bead.
- A dedicated E2E/integration or explicitly named higher-level suite exercises the user-facing or integration-facing surface touched by this bead, with structured logs, artifacts, and exact execution commands captured under the shared contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"This task should also flush out any copy-pasted timestamp helpers nearby so the codebase converges on one truth source instead of multiple “temporary” implementations.

## Verification Baseline
- Follow the shared verification and artifact contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Record exact commands, artifact paths, and if applicable `rch` worker identity in the completion evidence for this bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:30:08.555682Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T03:39:07.320684Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T03:39:07.320454Z","close_reason":"Replaced chrono_stub_now() with utc_now_iso8601() using chrono::Utc::now(). Replaced format_epoch_ms() hand-rolled calendar with chrono::DateTime::from_timestamp_millis(). 4 new tests (rfc3339 validity, millis, epoch zero, known date). All pass on rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.3.1","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:30:08.555682Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2752,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.3.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"This is a small but high-value cleanup because timestamps feed diagnostics and auditability. An approximate stub is exactly the wrong thing to leave in operator-visible output.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:30:08Z"},{"id":2778,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.3.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Detailed Verification Plan
- Proposed unit tests:
  - timestamp format stability
  - UTC/local expectations and boundary cases around date rollover
  - parsing/round-trip where applicable
- Proposed E2E script: `scripts/e2e_cli_timestamp_contract.sh`
  - verify operator-visible timestamps in live CLI output and backup/session metadata
- Required logs/artifacts:
  - environment timezone, format selection, output snapshots, exact commands","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:47Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.3.2","title":"Graduate env-bootstrap and ringlog from stub/no-op behavior to real bootstrap logging","description":"`frankenterm/env-bootstrap` currently describes itself as a minimal stub, and `setup_logger()` in `ringlog.rs` is explicitly a no-op.

This task owns making that crate either genuinely useful or explicitly narrow in a way that no longer pretends to provide bootstrap logging behavior.","design":"1. Audit who calls `env-bootstrap` today and what guarantees they expect.
2. Decide the minimal real bootstrap responsibility: tracing subscriber init, ring-buffer logging, env parsing, or a deliberate narrower contract.
3. Implement real side effects for `setup_logger()` or replace the exported API with something honest and smaller.
4. Remove module docs/comments that call the crate a stub once the behavior is real.","acceptance_criteria":"- `setup_logger()` is no longer a no-op placeholder on supported paths.
- Module-level docs accurately describe real behavior and limitations.
- Callers either receive real bootstrap logging or are updated to stop depending on the old fake contract.
- Tests validate initialization behavior and repeated-init safety where applicable.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover the happy path, edge/boundary cases, explicit error/failure/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability relevant to this bead.
- A dedicated E2E/integration or explicitly named higher-level suite exercises the user-facing or integration-facing surface touched by this bead, with structured logs, artifacts, and exact execution commands captured under the shared contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"Rule 0.5 applies: this is about completing the crate, not deleting it or folding it away. Keep the crate in the workspace.

Added env_logger dependency. Updated all docs to reflect real behavior. - Proposed E2E script: `scripts/e2e_env_bootstrap_logging.sh`
  - start the relevant binary entrypoints and verify bootstrap logs land in the expected sink or ring buffer
- Required logs/artifacts:
  - bootstrap phase markers, sink path, init-attempt count, redacted startup logs","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:47Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-akx00.4","title":"[EPIC] Replace connector and search placeholder implementations","description":"Own audited placeholders in connector trust/certification and semantic-search quality.

This track covers places where the current code claims verification, certification, or ranking behavior but actually performs a permissive no-op, a silent pass-through, or an always-failing placeholder implementation.","design":"Split the work into two principles:
- trust surfaces must never silently over-accept,
- retrieval quality surfaces must never silently advertise models or ranking that do nothing.

Where a backend is not worth keeping, remove the dead path instead of preserving a fake capability.","acceptance_criteria":"- Connector trust and certification phases do real work or explicitly fail closed.
- Search/reranking/embedder code no longer contains silent no-op model paths.
- Tests cover both configured and unavailable-backend behavior.
- Every concrete child bead under this epic closes with comprehensive unit tests plus a dedicated or explicitly assigned E2E/integration scenario, all executed under the shared verification contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"Primary files: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_registry.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_sdk.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/reranker.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/model2vec_embedder.rs`, `crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/fastembed_embedder.rs`.

## Track Verification Emphasis
- fixture-heavy verification with score/evidence capture, certification traces, and explicit unavailable-backend diagnostics.
- Heavy validation runs in this track must capture structured logs, exact command transcripts, and artifact paths.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:56.664785Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:29.723041Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connector","e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","search","stub-audit","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:56.664785Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2744,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"This track is partly about security posture and partly about epistemic honesty. A verification or reranking path that quietly does nothing is worse than an explicit "unsupported" error because it invites false confidence.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:28:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-akx00.4.1","title":"Replace connector transparency verification stub with real verification policy","description":"`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_registry.rs::check_transparency()` is currently documented as a stub and effectively treats any non-empty token as verified.

This task owns replacing that permissive placeholder with a real verification policy so connector trust decisions are evidence-based rather than string-presence-based.","design":"1. Define what “transparency” means for this registry: signature chain, log inclusion proof, allowlist, remote attestation, or another verifiable artifact.
2. Implement verification so acceptance is tied to actual proof material.
3. Fail closed when verification material is missing or invalid.
4. Preserve actionable diagnostics so connector authors know why verification failed.","acceptance_criteria":"- A non-empty token alone is no longer sufficient to mark a connector as verified.
- Verification failure modes are explicit and test-covered.
- Comments/docs stop describing the function as a stub.
- Security-sensitive callers cannot accidentally treat placeholder verification as real trust.
- Comprehensive unit tests cover the happy path, edge/boundary cases, explicit error/failure/cancellation modes, and repeat-run stability relevant to this bead.
- A dedicated E2E/integration or explicitly named higher-level suite exercises the user-facing or integration-facing surface touched by this bead, with structured logs, artifacts, and exact execution commands captured under the shared contract in `ft-akx00.8`.","notes":"This task should define the canonical trust primitive that any later certification/integration probe can build on.

## Verification Baseline
- Follow the shared verification and artifact contract in `ft-akx00.8`.
- Record exact commands, artifact paths, and if applicable `rch` worker identity in the completion evidence for this bead.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:31:12.568576Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-03T03:28:23.660686Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","placeholder-remediation","quality-gate","stub-audit","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","depends_on_id":"ft-akx00.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:31:12.568576Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2755,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Silent over-acceptance is the dangerous failure mode here. If the system cannot verify a connector, it must say so plainly and fail closed.","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:31:12Z"},{"id":2781,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"## Detailed Verification Plan
- Proposed unit tests:
  - valid vs invalid proof material
  - missing material fail-closed behavior
  - diagnostic message stability
- Proposed E2E script: `scripts/e2e_connector_transparency_verification.sh`
  - run good/bad connector registrations through the verification path and snapshot decisions
- Required logs/artifacts:
  - connector id, proof source, decision, failure reason, evidence snapshot with secret-safe redaction","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:04:48Z"},{"id":2800,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"BeigeBluff","text":"Claimed by BeigeBluff. Implemented fail-closed transparency verification in connector_registry using structured proof tokens plus trusted transparency-root allowlisting, updated trust-policy builder support, and added unit coverage for malformed/missing/untrusted proof material plus reverify/install enforcement. First rch compile exposed a MerkleProof comparability gap for the new token type; fixed by deriving PartialEq/Eq on ProofStep and MerkleProof. Remote verification currently in progress via rch cargo test -p frankenterm-core transparency_valid_token -- --nocapture before widening to connector_registry_transparency_integration and scoped check/clippy.","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:02:35Z"},{"id":2801,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"WindyDesert","text":"Follow-up from WindyDesert while Agent Mail is intermittently failing:

I found an additional fail-open path after the transparency-token implementation landed: `ConnectorRegistryClient::register_package()` and `reverify_package()` only ran `check_transparency()` when `config.enforce_transparency` was true. If `trust_policy.require_transparency_proof` was true but `enforce_transparency` was false, the policy gate only enforced token presence and did not validate the proof material.

Current working-tree changes on my side add a shared `transparency_verification_required()` gate, route both registration and reverification through it, and add focused unit/integration coverage for the policy-required-proof case plus reverification. Remote verification currently running via `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test connector_registry_transparency_integration`.
","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:03:26Z"},{"id":2803,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"WindyDesert","text":"Verification update:

- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test connector_registry_transparency_integration` passed on worker `vmi1152480` at 2026-04-03T03:13Z.
- Result: 2/2 integration tests passed (`connector_registry_transparency_integration_policy_required_proof_fails_closed`, `connector_registry_transparency_integration_policy_required_proof_accepts_valid_token`).
- Remote runtime reported `Finished test profile [unoptimized + debuginfo] target(s) in 5m 01s`; end-to-end RCH wall time was ~416.6s including sync/artifact roundtrip.

I am continuing with additional `rch` cargo gates from here.
","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:13:32Z"},{"id":2804,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"WindyDesert","text":"Workspace verification update:

- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` reached a non-bead blocker in `frankenterm/window/src/os/xdg_desktop_portal.rs:116` on worker `vmi1152480`.
- Error: `CachedAppearance::Some(appearance)` expected `Appearance` but the current code passes `&Appearance`; compiler suggests `*appearance`.
- The run also emitted unrelated warnings under `frankenterm/window/src/connection.rs` and `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs`.

This means the full workspace gate is currently red for reasons outside `connector_registry`. I am following up with a crate-scoped clippy pass on `frankenterm-core` to keep verification relevant to this bead.
","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:21:01Z"},{"id":2805,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"BeigeBluff","text":"Coordination fallback note: Agent Mail health is green, but `fetch_inbox`, `list_agents`, and `send_message` are still intermittently failing with transient DB errors on my side. I reviewed WindyDesert's bead comments and confirmed the current tree already includes the shared `transparency_verification_required()` path plus the new integration surface at `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/connector_registry_transparency_integration.rs`, so I am avoiding overlapping code edits and focusing on scoped verification + evidence capture. Current evidence: my `rch` bundle at `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.4.1/scoped-verification/20260403T031146Z` already passed `cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib transparency -- --nocapture` on worker `vmi1227854`; the integration test step is still running there.","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:26:15Z"},{"id":2806,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"WindyDesert","text":"Additional verification update:

- `rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --tests -- -D warnings` is currently blocked by unrelated lint debt in `frankenterm/dynamic/derive/src/attr.rs:29` (`clippy::collapsible_match`).
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core transparency -- --nocapture` did not reach a transparency-specific failure; it stopped on pre-existing compile errors in `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/asupersync_spike.rs` (Select/Unpin and pattern-shape issues around lines 291-322).

Net: the new transparency integration lane is green, but broader crate/workspace verification remains blocked by unrelated existing failures outside this bead.
","created_at":"2026-04-03T03:27:46Z"},{"id":2807,"issue_id":"ft-akx00.4.1","author":"BeigeBluff","text":"Verification update: the scoped `rch` bundle at `artifacts/placeholder-remediation/ft-akx00.4.1/scoped-verification/20260403T031146Z` now has both focused test legs green on worker `vmi1227854`.
